Here the impacts of multiple investments/positions are stacked on each other. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string getInvestorImpacts parameters * JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation (example) 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description investor_identifier string yes String identifier for an investor asset_identifier string no For a specified asset of an investor reporting_year number/4 digits yes Year of the taxonomy decryption_key string no For decrypting personally identifiable information category_only number/integer no Default is all sub_category_only number/integer no Default is all super_indicator_only number/integer no Default is all indicator_only number/integer no Default is all decimals number/integer no Default is 0 decimals language_code string no Language codes as per getLanguages. Default is the original language. Example code curl getPortfolioImpacts Returns the impacts of an investor's portfolio of investments in green assets POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to retrieve the ESG impacts of a particular portfolio. Here the impacts of multiple investments/positions are consolidated when possibel (when the green framworks are the same). Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string getPortfolioImpacts parameters * JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation (example) 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description portfolio_identfier string yes String identifier for a portfolio asset_identifier string no For a specified asset in a portfolio reporting_year number/4 digits yes Year of the taxonomy decryption_key string no For decrypting personally identifiable information category_only number/integer no Default is all sub_category_only number/integer no Default is all super_indicator_only number/integer no Default is all indicator_only number/integer no Default is all decimals number/integer no Default is 0 decimals language_code string no Language codes as per getLanguages. Default is the original language. Example code curl calculatePositionImpacts Calculates and stores the impact of an investor's investment in a green asset POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to calculate the ESG impacts for a particular position already registered in ESG.Credits. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string calculatePositionImpacts parameters * JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description position_id number/integer yes Integer identifier for a position position_amount number yes Amount of money invested position_currency_code string yes Currency code of the investment, as per getCurrencies position_days number/integer no The term of the investment, default is 365 days reporting_year number/ 4 digits yes Year of the taxonomy Example code curl calculatePositionImpactsOnly Calculates and returns the impact of an investment in a green asset, without having to specify the investor and without storing the result POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to calculate the ESG impacts for a position without any data is stored or logged in ESG.Credits. This operation is applicable for cases when financial institutes wants to utilise ESG.Credits only for calculations and store and investor data in own data repositories. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string calculatePositionImpactsOnly parameters * JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation (example) 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description asset_identifier string yes String identifier for an asset position_amount number yes Amount of money invested position_currency_code string yes Currency code of the investment, as per getCurrencies position_days number/integer no The term of the investment, default is 365 days reporting_year number/4 digits yes Year of the taxonomy category_only number/integer no Default is all sub_category_only number/integer no Default is all super_indicator_only number/integer no Default is all indicator_only number/integer no Default is all decimals number/integer no Default is 0 decimals language_code string no Language codes as per getLanguages. Default is the original language. paragraphs: This section deals with the impact from the green assets, as per the particular investments made in them. Any impact data is removed when the associated position is removed.

Use this operation to retrieve the ESG impacts of a particular position. The operation can return either a JSON formatted data set, or PDF file based on your template which you can provide to your customer as an investor report.
check Operation parameters
integer identifier for a investment
For decrypting personally identifiable information
Default is 0 decimals
Language codes as per getLanguages. Default is the original language.
Input report template name
Use this operation to retrieve the ESG impacts of a particular investor. Here the impacts of multiple investments/positions are stacked on each other.

String identifier for an investor
For a specified asset of an investor
For decrypting personally identifiable information
Default is 0 decimals
Language codes as per getLanguages. Default is the original language.
Use this operation to retrieve the ESG impacts of a particular portfolio. Here the impacts of multiple investments/positions are consolidated when possibel (when the green framworks are the same).
check Operation parameters

For a specified asset in a portfolio
For decrypting personally identifiable information
Default is 0 decimals
Language codes as per getLanguages. Default is the original language.

position_currency_code

The term of the investment, default is 365 days

Use this operation to calculate the ESG impacts for a position without any data is stored or logged in ESG.Credits. This operation is applicable for cases when financial institutes wants to utilise ESG.Credits only for calculations and store and investor data in own data repositories.

pages.title: Investors | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: API operations for managing investors in the ESG Credits system, including creating investor profiles, generating random identifiers, encrypting personally identifiable information (PII), and removing investors. Supports encrypted storage of investor names and contact information with decryption key management.

codeExamples.description: Creates or updates an investor record with encrypted personally identifiable information using a generated encryption key
Generates a secure encryption key for encrypting and decrypting personally identifiable information in the ESG Credits repository

useCases: Register investors with encrypted personal information for privacy compliance
Generate random investor identifiers to avoid using PII as identifiers
Create encryption keys for securing investor names and email addresses
Retrieve investor lists with decrypted information using encryption keys
Remove investors and their associated data from the system

implementationSteps.title: Generate Encryption Key
implementationSteps.description: Call generateEncryptionKey to create a secure key for encrypting PII. Store this key safely as it's required for decryption

implementationSteps.title: Create Investor Profile
implementationSteps.description: Use addInvestor with investor_identifier, investor_name, investor_currency_code, investor_email, and encryption_key to register the investor

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve Investor Data
implementationSteps.description: Call getInvestors with decryption_key parameter to view investor information in readable format

pages.fullContent: BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube ESG Credits Solution Overview ESG Credits API Taxonomy Translations Currencies Frameworks Projects Issuers Assets Investors Positions Portfolios Impacts Blockchain Users X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy Marketplace Solutions ESG Credits ESG Credits API Investors This section has operations to manage investors, i.e. showing, adding, encrypting, and removing investors getInvestors Returns all investors POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to retrieve all investors. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string getInvestors parameters JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation (example) 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Copy [ { "investor_id" : 19 , "investor_identifier" : "3a6515809856" , "investor_name" : "John Smith" , "investor_currency_code" : "USD" , "investor_email" : " [email protected] " } , { "investor_id" : 20 , "investor_identifier" : "9c809eb86ee6" , "investor_name" : "Mary Smith" , "investor_currency_code" : "USD" , "investor_email" : " [email protected] " } ] Copy { "message" : "No investors are registered." } Copy { "message" : "Not authorized." } Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description depryption_key string no For decrypting personally identifiable information user_identifier string no For limiting the output to the investors that the user has access to Example code curl generateInvestorIdentifier Generates a random investor identifier POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to generate investor identifiers, for cases where they need to random and not personally identifiable information (PII). Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string generateInvestorIdentifier Example responses 200: OK Successful operation (example) 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Example code curl generateEncryptionKey Generates an encryption key used to encrypt personal identifiable information POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to generate an encryption, used to encrypt and decrypt personally identiable infomation (PII) in the data repository of ESG.Credits. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string generateEncryptionKey Example responses 200: OK Successful operation (example) 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Example code curl addInvestor Creates and updates the information of an existing investor POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to create investors stored in ESG.Credits. Is also used to alter already existing investor data. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string addInvestor parameters * JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description investor_identifier string yes String identifier for an investor, potentially personally identifiable information (subject to encryption) investor_name string yes Personally identiable information (subject to encryption) investor_currency_code string yes Currency code of the investor, as per getCurrencies (currently not utilized) investor_email string no For investor correspondence e.g investor reports encryption_key string no For encrypting personally identifiable information Example code curl removeInvestor Removes an investor POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to remove investors from the ESG.Credits data repository. pages.title: Issuers | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: API operations for managing issuers of green assets such as green bonds and sustainable loans. Enables creating issuer profiles with public or private visibility, updating issuer information, retrieving issuer lists, and removing issuers from the ESG Credits data repository.

codeExamples.description: Creates or updates an issuer of green assets with identifier, name, currency, and visibility settings

useCases: Register banks and financial institutions as green bond issuers
Create issuer profiles for organizations offering sustainable assets
Set issuer visibility to public for cross-account accessibility
Update existing issuer information including currency preferences
Remove issuers no longer offering green assets

implementationSteps.title: Create Issuer Profile
implementationSteps.description: Use addIssuer with issuer_identifier, issuer_name, issuer_currency_code, and issuer_visibility to register the green asset issuer

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve Issuers
implementationSteps.description: Call getIssuers with optional issuer_visibility parameter to view public or private issuers in your account

implementationSteps.title: Remove Issuer
implementationSteps.description: Use removeIssuer with issuer_identifier to delete issuers that no longer have associated assets

Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string getIssuers parameters JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation (example) 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error messagee Copy [ { "issuer_id" : 1 , "issuer_identifier" : "35f0c1a1c08" , "issuer_name" : "X Bank" , "issuer_currency_code" : "EUR" , "issuer_visibility" : "public" } , { "issuer_id" : 6 , "issuer_identifier" : "good-bank-issuer" , "issuer_name" : "Good Bank" , "issuer_currency_code" : "SEK" , "issuer_visibility" : "private" } ] Copy { "message" : "No such issuer is registered." } Copy { "message" : "Not authorized." } Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description issuer_visibility string no Public or private. Default is private, meaning not visible across different BWS accounts. Example code curl addIssuer Creates or updates information about an existing issuer POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to add issuers of green assets. This operation also alters already existing issuer information. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string addIssuer parameters * JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description issuer_identifier string yes String identifier for an issuer issuer_name string yes Issuer name issuer_currency_code string yes Currency code of the asset, as per getCurrencies issuer_visibility string no Public or private. Default is private, meaning not visible across different BWS accounts. Example code curl removeIssuer Removes an issuer POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to remove issuers of green assets from the ESG.Credits data repository. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string removeIssuer parameters * JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description issuer_identifier string yes String identifier for an issuer Example code curl Previous Projects Next Assets Last updated 1 month ago Was this helpful? getIssuers Returns private or public issuers addIssuer Creates or updates information about an existing issuer removeIssuer Removes an issuer Was this helpful? Copy curl --location 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call' \ --header 'X-Api-Key: API-KEY' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --data '{ "solution": "BWS.ESG.Credits", "operation": "getIssuers", "parameters": { "issuer_visibility": "public" } } Copy { "message": "Issuer was successfully added." } Copy { "message": "Invalid issuer visibility term used." } Copy { "message": "Not authorized." } Copy curl --location 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call' \ --header 'X-Api-Key: API-KEY' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --data '{ "solution": "BWS.ESG.Credits", "operation": "addIssuer", "parameters": { "issuer_identifier": "good_issuer", "issuer_name": "Good Bank", "issuer_currency_code": "EUR", "issuer_visibility": "private" } } Copy { "Issuer was successfully removed." } Copy { "message": "Issuer was not removed. Asset(s) exist." } Copy { "message": "Not authorized." } Copy curl --location 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call' \ --header 'X-Api-Key: API-KEY' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --data '{ "solution": "BWS.ESG.Credits", "operation": "removeIssuer", "parameters": { "issuer_identifier": "good_issuer" }

pages.title: Portfolios | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: API operations for managing investor portfolios containing multiple green asset investments. Supports creating portfolios, adding and removing positions within portfolios, retrieving portfolio compositions, and consolidating impact data across portfolio holdings for comprehensive ESG reporting.

codeExamples.description: Creates a new portfolio for an investor or updates an existing portfolio identifier
Associates a specific investment position with an investor's portfolio for consolidated impact tracking

useCases: Create green investment portfolios for individual investors
Group multiple green asset positions into managed portfolios
Retrieve all investments within a specific portfolio
Consolidate ESG impact data across portfolio holdings
Remove positions from portfolios before deleting investments

implementationSteps.title: Create Portfolio
implementationSteps.description: Use addPortfolio with investor_identifier and portfolio_identifier to establish a new portfolio container for green investments

implementationSteps.title: Add Positions
implementationSteps.description: Call addPositionToPortfolio with portfolio_identifier and position_id to associate existing investments with the portfolio

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve Portfolio Data
implementationSteps.description: Use getPortfolioPositions to view all investments in a portfolio or getPortfolios to see all portfolios with their holdings

Default is all. user_identifier string no For limiting the output to the investors that the user has access to decryption_key string no For decrypting personally identifiable information Example code curl getPortfolioPositions Returns all investments in green assets in a portfolio of an investor POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to retrieve all green investments in an investors portfolio. Impacts can then be consolidated on portfolio level. pages.title: Positions | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: API operations for managing investment positions in green assets, including creating investor investments, updating position details, and removing positions. Positions represent specific amounts invested in green assets with associated currencies, terms, and start dates used for impact calculations.

codeExamples.description: Creates or updates an investment position for an investor in a green asset with amount, currency, term, and start date

useCases: Register investor investments in green bonds and sustainable assets
Track investment amounts, currencies, and investment terms
Create positions that automatically calculate environmental impacts
Retrieve all investments made by a specific investor
Remove closed or expired investment positions and their impact data

implementationSteps.title: Create Investment Position
implementationSteps.description: Use addPosition with investor_identifier, asset_identifier, position_amount, position_currency_code, and optional reporting_year to register the investment

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve Positions
implementationSteps.description: Call getPositions with investor_identifier and optional filters for asset_identifier or issuer_identifier to view investments

implementationSteps.title: Remove Position
implementationSteps.description: Use removePosition with investor_identifier and position_id to delete investments and their associated impact records

String identifier for a specified issuer to include. Default is all.
For limiting the output to the investors that the user has access to
For decrypting personally identifiable information

position_currency_code

pages.title: Projects | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: API operations for managing green projects that contribute to environmental frameworks. Includes creating projects, defining project-specific impact indicators and categories, consolidating projects into frameworks, and managing project visibility with multi-year reporting support for green project portfolios.

codeExamples.description: Creates a new green project and associates it with a framework for impact consolidation

useCases: Register renewable energy projects like wind parks and solar farms
Define project-specific environmental impact metrics and indicators
Consolidate multiple projects into green bond frameworks
Track allocated funding amounts per project by reporting year
Build frameworks from project data for comprehensive reporting

implementationSteps.title: Create Project
implementationSteps.description: Use addProject with project_identifier, project_name, project_currency_code, framework_identifier, and project_visibility to register the green project

implementationSteps.title: Add Year-Specific Details
implementationSteps.description: Call addYearSpecificsToProject with allocated_amount and reporting_year to set annual funding data for the project

implementationSteps.title: Define Impact Indicators
implementationSteps.description: Use addCategoryIndicatorToProject to specify sub_category_id, indicator_id, indicator_value, and sub_category_volume for environmental benefits

implementationSteps.title: Consolidate into Framework
implementationSteps.description: Call buildFrameworkFromProjects with framework_identifier and reporting_year to aggregate all associated project impacts into a unified framework

pages.title: Taxonomy | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: API operations for building and managing ESG taxonomy structures including categories, sub-categories, indicators, and units. Supports UN Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) and EU Environmental Objectives integration, with multi-language capabilities and year-specific taxonomy configurations for environmental impact measurement.

codeExamples.description: Adds a new environmental impact category to the taxonomy for a specific reporting year

useCases: Build custom ESG taxonomy with environmental categories and indicators
Associate UN SDGs with taxonomy sub-categories for alignment reporting
Link EU Environmental Objectives to green asset classifications
Define measurement units for impact indicators like CO2e kg or MWh
Retrieve taxonomy components in multiple languages for international reporting

implementationSteps.title: Add Categories
implementationSteps.description: Use addCategory with category_name, reporting_year, and language_code to create main environmental classification categories

implementationSteps.title: Create Sub-Categories
implementationSteps.description: Call addSubCategory with sub_category_name, category_id, reporting_year, and language_code to define specific impact areas

implementationSteps.title: Associate SDGs and Objectives
implementationSteps.description: Use addGoalObjectiveToSubCategory with sub_category_id, sdg_id, and eu_objective_id to link UN goals and EU objectives

implementationSteps.title: Define Indicators and Units
implementationSteps.description: Add measurement indicators and units to complete the taxonomy structure for impact quantification

Example code curl addCategory Adds and updates a category to the taxonomy for a particular reporting year POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to add a category to your taxonomy. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string addCategory parameters * JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation (example) 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description category_name string yes Category name to add or change reporting_year number/4 digits yes Year of the taxonomy category_name_changed string no New category name language_code string yes Selection of original language code as per getLanguages Example code curl addSubCategory Adds and updates a sub category to the taxonomy for a particular reporting year POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to add a sub category to your taxonomy. pages.title: Translations | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Provides API operations for managing multilingual translations of ESG taxonomy elements including categories, sub-categories, indicators, and units. Supports 12 languages to enable international ESG impact reporting across different regions and frameworks.

codeExamples.description: Retrieves all available languages for ESG taxonomy translations
Adds Swedish translation for a category in the 2023 taxonomy
Translates an indicator into Swedish for year 2022

useCases: Provide multilingual ESG reports for international investors
Translate environmental indicators into local languages for regional compliance
Create localized sustainability dashboards for different markets
Support multi-country ESG frameworks with language-specific taxonomy
Enable cross-border green bond reporting in native languages

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve Available Languages
implementationSteps.description: Call getLanguages operation to get all supported language codes (English, Swedish, Danish, Norwegian, Finnish, German, Polish, French, Dutch, Spanish, Portuguese, Italian)

implementationSteps.title: Add Translations for Taxonomy Elements
implementationSteps.description: Use addCategoryTranslation, addSubCategoryTranslation, addSuperIndicatorTranslation, addIndicatorTranslation operations with language_code and translated phrases

implementationSteps.title: Translate Units
implementationSteps.description: Add unit translations using addUnitTranslation for each reporting year, even if units are identical across languages

pages.title: Users | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Manages end users, user groups, and investor access permissions within the ESG Credits platform. Enables role-based access control, group assignments, and linking users to specific investors for controlled access to ESG investment data and reporting.

codeExamples.description: Creates a new user with encrypted personal information
Assigns a user to the bankers group for role-based access
Grants a user access to specific investor portfolio data

useCases: Control which financial advisors can access specific client ESG portfolios
Assign bankers to investor groups for personalized green investment reports
Manage venture capitalist access to green project sponsor data
Restrict user access to ESG impact data based on investor relationships
Organize users into regulatory, exchange, and issuer groups for compliance

implementationSteps.title: Create Users
implementationSteps.description: Use addUser operation with user_identifier, personal details, role (user or admin), and optional encryption_key for sensitive data

implementationSteps.title: Assign Groups
implementationSteps.description: Call addGroupToUser to connect users to groups (bankers, investors, capitalists, issuers, agents, exchanges, regulators) for access control

implementationSteps.title: Link Investors to Users
implementationSteps.description: Use addInvestorToUser to grant users permission to view specific investor portfolios and ESG impact reports

pages.title: Solution Overview | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Overview of BWS ESG Credits solution designed for financial institutions to add ESG impact reporting to digital channels and investment platforms. Supports green asset impact calculations per ICMA framework with multilingual taxonomy, blockchain certificates, and double-counting prevention.
useCases: Banks providing ESG impact reports to retail customers in digital banking channels
Investment advisors creating portfolio-level green asset impact statements
Venture capitalists generating consolidated ESG reports for project sponsors
Green bond issuers publishing impact characteristics for promotional purposes
Certification bodies digitally publishing quality-assured green project frameworks

implementationSteps.title: Define ESG Taxonomy
implementationSteps.description: Create custom taxonomy with categories, indicators, and units aligned with ICMA or chosen framework, supporting multiple languages and UN SDGs

implementationSteps.title: Register Green Assets and Projects
implementationSteps.description: Add green frameworks and project benefit characteristics that form the basis for impact calculations per annum or instrument term

implementationSteps.title: Generate Impact Reports
implementationSteps.description: Calculate consolidated ESG benefits for investor portfolios, optionally storing impact profiles immutably on blockchain with certificates

pages.fullContent: BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube ESG Credits Solution Overview ESG Credits API X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy Marketplace Solutions ESG Credits Solution Overview BWS.ESG.Credits solution overview This API solution is designed for financial institutions that want to quickly add ESG impact benefits to their digital channels and investment reporting. The benefits come from investments made in green assets. Typical use cases include: Banks and investment advisors who want to create and provide investor reports to their broad customer base in digital channels, for the specific scope of green assets they market. Institutional investors in green assets who want to create and view investment reports for their own investment portfolio of broadly available green assets. Venture capitalists funding green projects that want to create and provide their sponsors with investor reports on both project and consolidated levels, for each sponsor’s investment mix. Issuers of green assets that want to provide banks, advisors, and investors with impact details for promotional purposes, and for use in impact calculations. Certification bodies and agents that want to digitally publish quality-assured impact characteristics of green projects and green frameworks to issuers of green assets. By utilizing this API solution, your development team can accelerate the development of your ESG impact reporting solution. The underlying information model and calculation methods are designed to make it easy for you to add impact reporting to your digital channels and investment reporting. Impact reporting can be done as per the ICMA framework or any taxonomy of choice. The solution supports two levels of impact categories and two levels of impact indicators, as well as any impact unit. Impact amounts are calculated per annum or as per the term of the financial instrument. The green frameworks' “impact profile” forms the basis for calculations. Benefit characteristics of the green projects invested in can be consolidated and form your green framework. You can tie United Nations SDGs and EU’s Environmental Objectives classifications to taxonomy impact categories as per your choice. Any combination of currencies are supported, and your choice of taxonomy language can be accessed interchangeable. The ESG impact profile of your framework can be immutably stored in a blockchain to demonstrate your committment. Blockchain certificates for your customers' specific investment in green assets can also be issued with help of ESG.Credits. Finally, the blockchain can be utilized to ensure that green project benefits are not double-counted across the debt capital community. paragraphs: BWS.ESG.Credits solution overview

This API solution is designed for financial institutions that want to quickly add ESG impact benefits to their digital channels and investment reporting. The benefits come from investments made in green assets. Typical use cases include:
Banks and investment advisors who want to create and provide investor reports to their broad customer base in digital channels, for the specific scope of green assets they market.
Institutional investors in green assets who want to create and view investment reports for their own investment portfolio of broadly available green assets.
Venture capitalists funding green projects that want to create and provide their sponsors with investor reports on both project and consolidated levels, for each sponsor’s investment mix.
Certification bodies and agents that want to digitally publish quality-assured impact characteristics of green projects and green frameworks to issuers of green assets.

By utilizing this API solution, your development team can accelerate the development of your ESG impact reporting solution. The underlying information model and calculation methods are designed to make it easy for you to add impact reporting to your digital channels and investment reporting.

Impact reporting can be done as per the ICMA framework or any taxonomy of choice. The solution supports two levels of impact categories and two levels of impact indicators, as well as any impact unit.

Impact amounts are calculated per annum or as per the term of the financial instrument. The green frameworks' “impact profile” forms the basis for calculations. Benefit characteristics of the green projects invested in can be consolidated and form your green framework.

You can tie United Nations SDGs and EU’s Environmental Objectives classifications to taxonomy impact categories as per your choice. Any combination of currencies are supported, and your choice of taxonomy language can be accessed interchangeable.

The ESG impact profile of your framework can be immutably stored in a blockchain to demonstrate your committment. Blockchain certificates for your customers' specific investment in green assets can also be issued with help of ESG.Credits. Finally, the blockchain can be utilized to ensure that green project benefits are not double-counted across the debt capital community.

pages.title: Calendar | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Defines Calendar object representing scheduled games, matches, or tournaments as time-bound events linked to Fields. Enables season-based, game-based, or tournament-based fan campaigns with operations to create, list, and delete calendar entries.

codeExamples.description: Creates a new calendar for the 2025-26 season and returns calendarId
Retrieves list of calendars or specific calendar by ID
Deletes a calendar by its calendarId

useCases: Create UEFA Champions League Final calendar for single match events
Schedule entire LaLiga 2025-2026 season with multiple match dates
Organize multi-week cricket tournament calendars with game schedules
Set up weekend chess tournament timelines for fan participation
Manage overlapping sports calendars for multi-sport fan platforms

implementationSteps.title: Create Calendar
implementationSteps.description: Call new_calendar with calendar name (e.g., 'LaLiga 2025-26') to get calendarId for linking matches

implementationSteps.title: Link to Field
implementationSteps.description: Associate calendar with Field objects to create scheduled match instances on specific sports venues

implementationSteps.title: Manage Calendar Lifecycle
implementationSteps.description: Use list_calendars to view calendars and delete_calendar to remove outdated schedules

pages.title: Cubes | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents Cubes objects representing tokenized zones or tiles within a Field. Each Cube is a unique NFT owned by fans, tracking ownership, enabling rewards, and reflecting live event impacts during matches with pricing management capabilities.

codeExamples.description: Lists first 5 cubes for a field with pagination support
Updates pricing for specific cubes to $5.00 USD using i_j index format

useCases: Sell NFT field zones to fans for ownership during matches
Update cube pricing based on field position importance (center vs corners)
Track cube ownership and rewards for live match events
Paginate through hundreds of cubes efficiently for large stadiums
Manage dynamic pricing for premium field locations

implementationSteps.title: Auto-Generate Cubes
implementationSteps.description: Cubes are automatically created when a new Field is created, divided by cubeLength parameter

implementationSteps.title: List and Identify Cubes
implementationSteps.description: Use list_cubes with fieldId and limit to retrieve cubes with their coordinates (x, y) and indexes (i, j)

implementationSteps.title: Set Cube Pricing
implementationSteps.description: Call update_cube-price with cubesList using i_j format and priceInCents to set USD pricing

pages.fullContent: BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube FAN Game Overview FAN Game Cube API Calendar Field Cubes Plays (Field-Calendar) Match Prizes Event Types Live Events ESG Credits X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy Marketplace Solutions FAN Game Cube FAN Game Cube API Cubes Represents a zone or tile within a Field . Each Cube is a unique NFT owned by a fan. It tracks ownership, enables rewards, and reflects live event impacts during matches. Cubes are generated automatically when creating a new field. list_cubes POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string list_cubes parameters * JSON check method parameters new_field Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption fieldId string Field Id. limit number The maximum number of cubes returned (a field can have hundreds of cubes). lastEvaluatedKey (optional) If more cubes are available, include the returned key to get the next chink of cubes. list_cubes API Call Example list_cubes API call using Node.js Copy const axios = require ( 'axios' ); /* build request to use BWS Badges solution */ const request = { { "solution" : "BWS.NFT.GameCube" , "operation" : "list_cubes" , "parameters" : { "fieldId" : "5a1486f3-753c-4194-b2d4-ea68b6e420c2" , "limit" : 5 } } /* call BWS API using Axios */ let config = { method : 'post' , maxBodyLength : Infinity , url : 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call' , headers : { 'X-Api-Key' : 'XqaLg...... A5k2V729v' , /* use your API key here! */ 'Content-Type' : 'application/json' } , data : JSON .stringify (request) }; axios.request(config) .then((response) => { console .log ( JSON .stringify ( response .data)); }) .catch((error) => { console .log (error); }); list_cubes Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns a list of cubes and their related cube data, including the cubeId . If more cubes are available, the field lastEvaluatedKey is returned (include it on the API call for getting the next chunk of cubes). update_cube-price POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string update_cube-price parameters JSON check method parameters update_cube-price Method Parameters (optional) Parameter Type Desciption field_id string Field Id cubesList list A list of cubes whose price needs to be updated. Use cube data i and j indexes to identify each cube using the format i_j (e.g. 40_0). priceInCents number Price in USD cents (e.g. 500 to indicate a 5USD cube price) update_cube-price API Call Example new_field API call using Node.js update_cube-price Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the number of correctly updated cubes, the number of invalid detected cubes, and the list of invalid cubes. pages.title: Event Types | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Defines Event Types representing in-game occurrences like goals, assists, or plays registered with spatial coordinates and timestamps. Events affect cube owners whose zones match action locations with configurable point systems for scoring.

Set up assist event types for collaborative scoring mechanics
Configure foul events with negative points for penalty zones
Design custom event types for different sports (chess moves, cricket wickets)

implementationSteps.title: Create Event Types
implementationSteps.description: Use new_event-type with fieldId, eventTypeName, and eventTypePoints to define scorable actions

implementationSteps.title: Configure Point System

implementationSteps.title: Update or Remove Events
implementationSteps.description: Use update_event-type to adjust points or delete_event-type to remove unused event types

pages.title: Field | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents Field object as the foundational virtual sports venue tokenized into interactive cube zones. Represents real-world stadiums with configurable dimensions and sports types, serving as the canvas for all game interactions and NFT cube generation.

codeExamples.description: Creates Camp Nou field (120m x 80m) divided into 3m x 3m cubes
Retrieves field details by fieldId or lists all fields
Deletes field and all associated cubes permanently

useCases: Create digital representation of Real Madrid Stadium for match gameplay
Tokenize Madison Square Garden into interactive zones for basketball
Set up virtual cricket field with customizable dimensions
Design multi-sport venues supporting different field sizes
Generate NFT cubes automatically for fan ownership and rewards

implementationSteps.title: Define Field Parameters
implementationSteps.description: Specify field name, sport type, dimensions in centimeters (fieldLength, fieldWidth), and cubeLength for zone size

implementationSteps.title: Create Field
implementationSteps.description: Call new_field operation which automatically generates all cubes based on dimensions and cubeLength parameters

implementationSteps.title: Link to Matches
implementationSteps.description: Use returned fieldId to associate with Calendar objects for scheduled match events and gameplay

pages.fullContent: BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube FAN Game Overview FAN Game Cube API Calendar Field Cubes Plays (Field-Calendar) Match Prizes Event Types Live Events ESG Credits X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy Marketplace Solutions FAN Game Cube FAN Game Cube API Field Represents a virtual sports field tokenized into interactive zones or sections ("CUBES"). It is the foundational canvas for all game interactions. Each field is typically linked to a real-world sports match. Examples: Real Madrid Stadium Madison Square Garden new_field POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string new_field parameters * JSON check method parameters new_field Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption name string Field name (e.g. Camp Nou) sport string Sport (e.g. Football) fieldLength number Field length in centimeters (e.g. 12000) fieldWidth number Field width in centimeters (e.g. 9000) cubeLength number The square size of a single cube (e.g. 300 for a cube of 3x3 meters) new_field API Call Example new_field API call using Node.js new_field Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the fieldId , which will be used to link NFT Game Cube related objects. All the cubes for that field are created when creating a new field. list_fields POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string list_fields parameters JSON (optional) check method parameters list_fields Method Parameters (optional) Parameter Type Desciption field_id string Field Id list_fields API Call Example new_field API call using Node.js list_fields Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the list of fields you created or a single field if fieldId parameter is included in the call. delete_field POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string delete_field parameters * JSON check method parameters delete_field Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption fieldId string Field Id to delete. delete_field API Call Example delete_field API call using Node.js delete_field Call Response When the API call is executed without errors, it returns a successful status code. All the cubes created and assigned to the deleted field are also deleted. pages.title: Live Events | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents LiveEvent API for recording in-match events at specific field coordinates during scheduled matches. Serves as the primary mechanism to capture game data (goals, moves, fouls) for mapping to cubes, supporting manual entry, AI detection, and application integration.

codeExamples.description: Records a live event at coordinates (45, 40) on the field during a match

useCases: Record goal events manually via organizer dashboard during live matches
Capture AI-detected game events in real-time using machine learning models
Track chess moves automatically with coordinates for tournament gameplay
Register fouls and passes programmatically through application integration
Map game actions to cube owners for scoring and reward distribution

implementationSteps.title: Prepare Match Context
implementationSteps.description: Gather matchId, fieldId, and calendarId for the scheduled game where event occurs

implementationSteps.title: Record Event with Coordinates
implementationSteps.description: Call new_event with event object containing x/y coordinates and eventTypeId to capture game action location

implementationSteps.title: Process Rewards
implementationSteps.description: System automatically maps event coordinates to affected cubes and awards points to cube owners based on event type

It is the primary mechanism by which game data—such as goals, moves, fouls, or plays—is captured and mapped to corresponding Cubes for scoring and reward processing. This API serves as a trusted interface to power fan engagement: Manually via the organizer dashboard or solution UI (BWS Solution Interface) Automatically via BWS-provided machine learning models that detect and push game events in real time (e.g., AI-driven goal recognition, chess move capture) Through your application. new_event POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string new_event parameters * JSON check method parameters new_event Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption matchId string The Match Id for which you want to register the event. fieldId string The Field Id the match is linked to. calendarId string The calendar Id the match is linked to. event JSON check event parameter new_event Event Parameter Parameter Type Desciption coordinates JSON check coordinates parameter eventTypeId string The Event Type Id of the event you want to register. new_event Coordinates Parameter Parameter Type Desciption x string The 2D x coordinate of the event. y string The 2D y coordinate of the event. new_event API Call Example new_event API call using Node.js new_event Call Response When the API call is executed without errors, it returns a successful status code. pages.title: Match | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: API reference for managing Match objects in FAN Game Cube. Covers creating, updating, listing, and deleting time-bound gameplay sessions tied to specific fields and calendars. Includes endpoints for match scheduling, team configuration, and status management.

codeExamples.description: Creates a new match for a specific calendar with team details, start time, and match image stored in IPFS
Lists matches for a specific calendar, optionally filtering by matchId to retrieve specific match details
Deletes a specific match from a calendar by providing both matchId and calendarId

useCases: Schedule football matches like PSG vs Manchester City with team flags and images
Create chess tournament rounds like Magnus Carlsen vs Hikaru Nakamura
Track match status from scheduled to playing to finished or canceled
Set dynamic pricing for match cubes in USD cents
Update match details and descriptions during live events

implementationSteps.title: Create Match
implementationSteps.description: Call new_match operation with calendarId, startTimeInMillis, match name, team details, and base64 encoded images. Receives matchId in response.

implementationSteps.title: Update Match Status
implementationSteps.description: Use update_match operation with matchId and calendarId to modify match properties like status ('scheduled', 'playing', 'finished', 'canceled') or pricing.

implementationSteps.title: List and Monitor
implementationSteps.description: Retrieve match details using list_matches operation, optionally filtering by specific matchId to view IPFS URLs for images and current match data.

pages.fullContent: BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube FAN Game Overview FAN Game Cube API Calendar Field Cubes Plays (Field-Calendar) Match Prizes Event Types Live Events ESG Credits X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy Marketplace Solutions FAN Game Cube FAN Game Cube API Match A Match is a time-bound, gameplay session tied to a specific Field, during which events are tracked, points are awarded, and fan interactions occur. It represents a game instance—such as a football match, chess round, or tournament heat—scheduled via the Calendar object and hosted on a defined Field .\ Examples: PSG vs. Manchester City Green Bay Packers vs Chicago Bears Magnus Carlsen vs Hikaru Nakamura Images The images sent using base64 encoding are saved into IPFS using the BWS IPFS Solution. When getting object details, the IPFS URL is returned. new_match POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string new_match parameters * JSON check method parameters new_match Method Parameters Optionals All parameters are optional (except calendarId and startTimeInMillis). Parameter Type Desciption calendarId * string Calendar Id the match should be included in. startTimeInMillis * number The expected match start time in milliseconds. name string The match short name. description string Match description. image string An image base64 encoded string to announce the match. team1Name string The team name (e.g. Manchester City) team1Flag string An image base64 ecoded string representing the team flag. team2Name string The team name (e.g. LA Lakers) team2Flag string An image base64 ecoded string representing the team flag. status string The match status. Use one of the following values: "scheduled", "playing", "finished", "canceled". priceInCents number The default price in USD cents for all the cubes if no specific price is defined for a cube (e.g. 1000 for 10 USD). new_match API Call Example new_match API call using Node.js new_match Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the matchId for the newly created match. update_match POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string update_match parameters * JSON check method parameters update_match Method Parameters Optionals All parameters are optional (except calendarId and matchId) Only provide those you want to update. Parameter Type Desciption calendarId * string Calendar Id the match is included in. matchId * string The Match Id we want to update. startTimeInMillis number The expected match start time in milliseconds. name string The match short name. description string Match description. image string An image base64 encoded string to announce the match. team1Name string The team name (e.g. Manchester City) team1Flag string An image base64 ecoded string representing the team flag. team2Name string The team name (e.g. LA Lakers) team2Flag string An image base64 ecoded string representing the team flag. status string The match status. Use one of the following values: "scheduled", "playing", "finished", "canceled" priceInCents number The price in USD cents (e.g. 1000 for 10 USD) update_match API Call Example update_match API call using Node.js update_match Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the matchId for the updated match. list_matches POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string list_matches parameters JSON check method parameters list_matches Method Parameters (optional) Parameter Type Desciption calendar_id string The Calendar Id the match is linked to. matchId string (optional) The Match Id we want to list. list_matches API Call Example list_matches API call using Node.js list_matches Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the matches for the provided calendar. If we give a Match Id, it will just return the match we want to list. delete_match POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string delete_calendar parameters * JSON check method parameters delete_match Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption matchId string The Match Id of the match we want to delete. calendarId string The Calendar Id the match is linked to. delete_match API Call Example delete_match API call using Node.js delete_match Call Response When the API call is executed without errors, it returns a successful status code. Previous Plays (Field-Calendar) Next Prizes Last updated 1 month ago Was this helpful? new_match Request Body new_match API Call Example update_match Request Body update_match API Call Example list_matches Request Body list_matches API Call Example delete_match Request Body delete_match API Call Example Was this helpful? pages.title: Plays (Field-Calendar) | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: API reference for managing Field-Calendar relationships in NFT Game Cube. Documents how to link sports field configurations with tournament calendars, creating temporal lifecycles for matches. Includes operations for creating, listing, and deleting field-calendar associations with IPFS image storage.

codeExamples.description: Creates a relationship between a field and calendar, linking a sports ground's configuration to a specific tournament or season
Lists all calendars linked to a specific field, showing tournament names and IPFS image URLs
Response showing field-calendar relationship with IPFS-stored image URL for the tournament season

useCases: Link PSG's football field to their Champions League 2026 tournament calendar
Create New York Knicks 2026 NBA Season calendar for their basketball court
Manage multiple tournament calendars on the same sports field configuration
Track seasonal lifecycles of sports venues with temporal match scheduling
Store tournament branding images in IPFS for decentralized access

implementationSteps.title: Create Field-Calendar Link
implementationSteps.description: Call new_field-calendar with fieldId, calendarId, season description (e.g., 'PSG 2025-26'), and base64 encoded image. Image automatically saved to IPFS.

implementationSteps.title: List Field Calendars
implementationSteps.description: Use list_field-calendars with fieldId to retrieve all associated tournament calendars and their IPFS image URLs.

implementationSteps.title: Delete Relationship
implementationSteps.description: Remove field-calendar association using delete_field-calendar operation with both fieldId and calendarId parameters.

pages.title: Prizes | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: API reference for managing Prize objects in FAN Game Cube matches. Documents how to create, list, update, and delete rewards for cube performance. Supports configurable prizes including NFTs, tokens, physical goods, and experiences with multi-image support and HTML descriptions.

codeExamples.description: Creates a first-place prize for a match with name, description, and snapshot image stored in IPFS
Lists all prizes configured for a specific match, returning prize details with IPFS URLs
Updates prize details with HTML description for rich formatting and enhanced presentation

useCases: Award signed merchandise like Foden signed frames to top cube owners
Distribute VIP match tickets as prizes for first place winners
Offer Zoom calls or AMA sessions with athletes as exclusive rewards
Provide bonus $BWS tokens to incentivize cube ownership and engagement
Create three-tier prize systems with different rewards for positions 1, 2, and 3

implementationSteps.title: Create Prize
implementationSteps.description: Use new_match-prize with matchId, calendarId, position (1/2/3), name, description, and snapshot image. Optionally add htmlDescription and up to 3 additional images.

implementationSteps.title: List Match Prizes
implementationSteps.description: Call list_match-prizes with matchId to retrieve all configured prizes with their IPFS image URLs and positions.

implementationSteps.title: Update or Delete
implementationSteps.description: Modify prize details using update_match-prize with prizeId, or remove prizes entirely with delete_match-prize operation.

pages.title: FAN Game Overview | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Comprehensive overview of FAN Game Cube platform for building revenue streams in sports. Details multi-sport cube allocation, NFT ownership, dynamic pricing strategies, and real-time fan engagement. Covers implementation across football, chess, basketball, cricket, and other sports with tokenized field sections.
useCases: Divide football stadiums into 500-2000 cubes for premium zones like penalty boxes
Tokenize chess tournament 64 squares for unique event interaction
Create basketball court cubes covering three-point arc and paint zone
Launch weekend chess tournament with cube purchase via one-click widget
Generate $250,000 revenue with 500 cubes at $500 average price for VIP events
Enable fans to trade cubes mid-season on secondary marketplaces for royalties

implementationSteps.title: Define Cube Allocation
implementationSteps.description: Determine cube count (500, 1000, or 2000) and field division strategy based on sport. Assign premium pricing to high-value areas like penalty spots or center court.

implementationSteps.title: Configure Pricing Strategy
implementationSteps.description: Set dynamic pricing with higher rates for prime field areas and lower rates ($10 or less) for secondary zones to encourage broad participation.

implementationSteps.title: Integrate Purchase Widget
implementationSteps.description: Embed one-click JavaScript widget on club website for frictionless credit card purchases. Fans can buy cubes as easily as regular tickets.

implementationSteps.title: Enable Real-Time Engagement
implementationSteps.description: Launch mobile app for fans to manage cube collections, track live matches, join leaderboards, and compete for prizes during gameplay sessions.

pages.fullContent: BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube FAN Game Overview FAN Game Cube API ESG Credits X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy Marketplace Solutions FAN Game Cube FAN Game Overview Building New Revenue Streams for the Sports Industry BWS's FAN Game Cube platform revolutionizes fan engagement by allowing sports enthusiasts to own digital assets linked to specific sections of a sports field. This extends beyond football to include chess, tennis, cricket, basketball, rugby, baseball, hockey, and any sport with a field that can be split into "cubes". As real-time events unfold in their selected area, fans earn points and unlock exciting rewards, all while creating new revenue opportunities for sports clubs, former athletes, influencers, and other key figures in the sports industry. Want to launch a FAN Game Cube event? Contact us here . Cube Allocation for Different Sports Sports organizers can define how many cubes to sell and how to divide their playing area based on the sport’s unique characteristics. A football/soccer field cubes division. Examples include: Football stadiums It is typically divided into 500, 1000, or 2000 cubes , ensuring key areas like penalty boxes and midfield zones hold premium value. Chess tournaments The 64 chessboard squares can be tokenized, offering fans a unique way to interact with their favorite chess events. Tennis courts Tokenizing the baseline, service boxes, and net area allows fans to engage in real-time match events. Cricket fields Dividing the pitch into batting zones, fielding areas, and boundary sections enhances fan participation. Basketball courts Cube allocation can include key areas like the three-point arc, free-throw line, and paint zone . Rugby fields Fans can purchase cubes covering try zones, scrums, and midfield sections . Baseball fields Key locations such as bases, outfield zones, and pitcher’s mound can be tokenized. And a long etcetera. NFT Technology for Ownership and Trading FAN Game Cube utilizes NFT technology to ensure verifiable ownership. However, the usage of NFT goes beyond ownership, allowing fans to trade their cubes as they would with traditional assets. During a season or tournament, cube owners can buy and sell cubes mid-season on secondary marketplaces, generating additional royalties for sports clubs and organizers. Since NFTs offer permanent and transparent ownership records , fans gain long-term value while clubs benefit from secondary sales. Pricing and Monetization Strategy Organizers have complete control over pricing, ensuring dynamic valuation based on strategic cube placement. Different pricing strategies include: Prime field areas Higher prices can be charged for high-demand sections, such as the center of a football pitch, penalty spots, or key cricket fielding positions. Secondary zones To encourage broad participation, less strategic locations, such as side areas or backfield positions, can be offered at $10 or lower. Dynamic pricing models Pricing can fluctuate based on match importance, demand, and historical engagement levels. Revenue Potential Your expected revenue is calculated as follows: Number of cubes × Average Price per cube = Total income For example, launching 500 cubes at an average price of $500 per cube on a VIP event can generate $250,000 in revenue. Larger stadiums, premium tournaments, or full-season FAN Game Cubes can scale this model to generate even higher revenues. Seamless Integration with One-Click Purchase Widget BWS provides a one-click purchase widget for frictionless transactions. Sports clubs do not need to create special accounts or navigate complex payment gateways—cubes can be sold in just a few hours. A weekend Chess Tournament NFT Game Cube event. The widget integrates easily into existing club websites, allowing fans to purchase cubes as simply as buying a regular game ticket. How Fans Can Participate and Win Once a sports club publishes its one-click buy widget , fans can easily purchase FAN Game Cubes using standard credit cards, making participation straightforward and accessible. One-click FAN Game Cube purchase experience This experience brings a new layer of engagement to the fan experience while giving them direct ownership of in-game digital assets. Real-Time Fan Engagement and Play & Win Mechanics NFT-based cubes offer fans an engaging way to experience their favorite sports, providing: True ownership of digital sports assets. Fans can buy, sell, or hold tradeable assets for future value (full-season FAN Game Cube). Passive earnings potential through resale royalties and long-term value accumulation. Access to real-time engagement through a dedicated mobile app. Once a cube is purchased, fans can: Manage their cube collection across multiple sports. Track upcoming matches in real time. Join live leaderboards to compete with other fans for top prizes. Why Choose FAN Game Cube? Multi-Sport Scalability Works across various sports, including football, cricket, chess, basketball, rugby, baseball, hockey, golf, Formula 1, and esports. It is flexible and adaptable to different event structures and fan bases. One-Click Buy Widget A JavaScript widget for effortless integration that enhances the club’s purchasing experience with quick, seamless transactions. Easy & Free to Launch Clubs and organizers can set up Fan Game Cube events using a simple three-step workflow or API calls. Flexible Event Calendars Supports single VIP games, full-season matches, and tournaments. It is scalable, from small local events like the Ohio Chess Tournament to major leagues like NBA teams. Real-Time Fan Engagement A comprehensive mobile app enables clubs to foster deep connections with fans during matches. Features include live-streaming for real-time game viewing and interactive chat to enhance fan engagement and discussion. BWS Fan Game Cube is the future of sports engagement, bridging the digital and real-world experiences for fans while unlocking new revenue opportunities for sports organizations. Get started today and transform the way fans interact with their favorite games! Previous FAN Game Cube Next FAN Game Cube API Last updated 26 days ago Was this helpful? Cube Allocation for Different Sports NFT Technology for Ownership and Trading Pricing and Monetization Strategy Seamless Integration with One-Click Purchase Widget How Fans Can Participate and Win Real-Time Fan Engagement and Play & Win Mechanics Why Choose FAN Game Cube? pages.fullContent:
BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube ESG Credits X Bot X Bot Overview X Bot Installation X Bot Commands Getting Support Powered by GitBook On this page Copy Marketplace Solutions X Bot X Bot Commands Last Updated: 2025-12-30 This documentation is automatically generated from source code. This reference lists all available commands for the X Bot. Commands are organized by functional category. Admin Only commands can only be executed by group administrators. Basic Commands /get_chatid (Admin Only) Returns the chat ID of the group where the command is executed. Useful for debugging and configuration purposes. Only group administrators can execute this command. Usage: Copy /get_chatid Parameters: None required Workflow: Execution Context: Groups only (checked via isValidTopic) Admin Verification: Requires group administrator privileges States: No state management required Flow: Single-step command execution returning chat ID User Messages: Success: "Chat ID: [chat_id_value]" Error (Non-admin): "Only admins can get the chat ID." Error (Invalid topic): "This command is not allowed in this topic." Error (Exception): Generic exception message from XBotMessages.getCommandExceptionMessage() /help Displays available bot commands and their descriptions. Shows different command sets based on whether the user is a group administrator (full command list) or regular user (limited command list). Usage: Parameters: None required Workflow: Execution Context: Groups only (checked via isValidTopic) Admin Check: Determines which command set to display States: No state management required Flow: Single-step command execution with different outputs based on admin status User Messages: Success (Admin): Full command list including cashtags management (/add_cashtags, /remove_cashtags, /clear_cashtags) and raid commands Success (Regular User): Limited command list with basic commands (/help, /buy, /subscription, /report) Error (Invalid topic): "This command is not allowed in this topic." Error (Exception): "An error occurred while processing the help command." /start Bot initialization command that handles different workflows based on context and user state. In groups, shows simple bot running message. In private messages, manages complex state-based workflows including payment confirmation, validation codes, and bot setup guidance. Error (Not in group): "This command can only be used in group chats." Error (Users not admin): Lists users who are not administrators Error (No users provided): Usage instructions Error (Too many admins): "You can designate a maximum of 10 admins." Error (Invalid topic): "This command is not allowed in this topic." Error (Exception): Generic exception message from XBotMessages.getCommandExceptionMessage() Subscription & Credits /buy Initiates the purchase process for X Bot Professional subscription. Handles complex multi-step workflow that starts in group chat and continues in private messages with Stripe integration. Creates checkout sessions for new purchases and manages subscription state transitions. Usage: Parameters: None required (command triggered action) Workflow: Execution Context: Both group and private messages (context-aware behavior) Group Flow: Checks PRO status → Creates checkout session → Sets user state → Sends private message Private Flow: Continues purchase process or provides subscription management States Managed: 'waiting_for_purchase': User has active checkout session Multi-step Workflow: Group command → Private message continuation → Stripe checkout → Payment confirmation User Messages: Success (Group - New Purchase): Creates Stripe checkout session and sends private message with payment link Success (Group - Already PRO): "You already have a PRO license for this group. Use /subscription to manage your subscription." Success (Private - Continue Purchase): Provides checkout link or subscription management options Error (Stripe Integration): Various Stripe-related error messages Error (State Management): "An error occurred while processing your request. Please try again." Error (Exception): Generic exception message from XBotMessages.getCommandExceptionMessage() /get_credits Displays the current credit usage and availability for the bot. Shows different information based on license type (FREE shows remaining credits, PRO shows unlimited usage). Credits are consumed when fetching posts from X (Twitter) for report generation. Usage: Parameters: None required Workflow: Execution Context: Both groups and private messages License Check: Determines display format (FREE vs PRO) States: No state management required Flow: Single-step command execution with license-aware response User Messages: Success (PRO): " [Month Year] - PRO Edition 
Consumed Credits: [number]" Success (FREE): " [Month Year] - Free Edition 
Consumed Credits: [number]
Remaining: [number]

💡 Upgrade to PRO for unlimited credits! Use /buy to upgrade." Error (Exception): Generic exception message from XBotMessages.getCommandExceptionMessage() /payment_cancelled Handles payment cancellation when users exit Stripe checkout without completing payment. This command is automatically triggered when users return from cancelled Stripe payment flows via redirect URLs. Cleans up user state and provides guidance for retrying. Usage: Parameters: None required (callback command from Stripe) Workflow: Execution Context: Private messages only (triggered after Stripe redirect) State Cleanup: Removes 'waiting_for_purchase' state to reset user workflow Recovery Guidance: Provides instructions for retrying purchase States: Deletes 'waiting_for_purchase' state Flow: Stripe cancel redirect → State cleanup → Retry guidance message User Messages: Success: "❌ Payment was cancelled.

💡 You can try again anytime by using the /buy command in your group." Error (Exception): Generic exception message from XBotMessages.getCommandExceptionMessage() /payment_success Handles successful payment confirmation after Stripe checkout completion. This command is automatically triggered when users return from successful Stripe payment flows via redirect URLs. Cleans up user state and provides confirmation messaging. Usage: Parameters: None required (callback command from Stripe) Workflow: Execution Context: Private messages only (triggered after Stripe redirect) State Cleanup: Removes 'waiting_for_purchase' state Webhook Integration: Works in conjunction with Stripe webhooks for license activation States: Deletes 'waiting_for_purchase' state Flow: Stripe success redirect → State cleanup → Confirmation message User Messages: Success: "🎉 Thank you for your payment! Your subscription is being activated and you'll receive confirmation shortly.

✅ You can now return to your group and start using PRO features." Error (Exception): Generic exception message from XBotMessages.getCommandExceptionMessage() /subscription Provides access to Stripe customer portal for subscription management including billing history, payment methods, and cancellation options. Handles both group and private message contexts with appropriate PRO license validation and customer portal URL generation. Usage: Parameters: None required (management command) Workflow: Execution Context: Both group and private messages (context-aware behavior) Group Flow: Validates PRO license → Checks ownership → Provides portal access or guidance Private Flow: Direct portal access for subscription management License Validation: Ensures group has active PRO subscription Ownership Check: Verifies user is subscription owner for management access States: No state management required Flow: Context detection → License validation → Ownership verification → Portal URL generation User Messages: Success (Group - Owner): Provides Stripe customer portal link for subscription management Success (Private - Owner): Direct portal access with management options Error (Group - No PRO): "This group doesn't have a PRO subscription. Use /buy to upgrade." Error (Group - Not Owner): "Only the subscription owner can manage the subscription. Please contact [owner] for assistance." Error (Private - No Subscription): "You don't have an active subscription. Use /buy to subscribe."
